How about
you?

So, oh, look
at that.

Give me just one second.
Ok.

Ok.

when is it?

May 5th through the eighth?

Hm.

I mean, like, I know sometimes we'll go to, like, na A or stuff like that.
But, um, me.

we're trying to get into more of them this year.

um, we've done, uh, there's Christy, we've done Optech a few times.

I think.

To tech.

So I think I'm, we'll probably go out there this year.
Nice.

I just want to get out and meet people that you've only really met behind Zoom.
So
I feel that.
Hi Christy.
Sorry.

No worries at all.

Awesome.

And does anyone have a hard stop right at 1030
or that's central?
So in 25?

Ok.
So thinking um agenda for the day.
Um So Chris and I have met um over to talk about our Google Business automation tool.

So wanted to jump on and chat with you guys.
Um ask some questions around what you're currently doing.
Show you the products that I think best fits that.
And then after this, we can see if it's something you guys are interested in, we can talk next steps from there.
Uh How does that sound?
Ok.
Anything else that you wanted to add to the agenda for the day?
I think that's good.
Ok, well,
so what uh what are you guys doing as far as advertising goes right now?
Uh Like what ils are you using?
How are you, how are you generating leads right now?

We also uh started dabbling recently with Google ad words.

and I mean, we're on like Apartment List, Zillow and some markets um kind of stuff like that.

How are all of them working for you,
Christy?
What do you think?

I I we've seen results with Google ad words, so I really like that as well.
Um So I think both of them work.
I mean apartments.com, it always depends on the level, you know, the platform that you're on in the, in the marketing area.
Yeah, over all.
Yes.

Do you have, so apartments.com List Zillow as far as the ILSS go.
Do you typically roll those out full portfolio?

Um It depends.
So like apartments.com, yes, we have it rolled out company wide.
I think there's only one property that does not have apartments.com because it's not necessary.
Um As far as other items like Google ad words.
Um Yes, we just do it.
We've just done it on a couple of distressed assets, not the entire portfolio.
Zillow we don't use um We have not found Zillow to be, you know, efficient for us.
Um We don't, we get a lot of leads but we don't get a lot of closed, you know, applications.
Um and we've had it at a couple of properties and tried it.
Um We're just not happy with that.
I think r.com, we have it a couple of properties also, but I'm not familiar with it.
It's not on my assets and R vision we have on a couple of properties too and I just rolled it out on one.
But again, it's not a portfolio.
I think apartments.com is really the only portfolio wide.
The other ones we will try out at certain properties like Google ad words, we tried it out, it did great.
So we rolled it out on, you know, a couple more.
Um We try not to just throw money at a problem.
Um We really are very thoughtful about that.
Yeah.

Yeah, I feel like I'm hearing more and more of that as I have these conversations with companies, like we typically, our strategy has been, let's get this, let's try this um source here, let's try this source here.
And there's just a lot of different like trialing um around the portfolio and I feel like more and more in the last really like six months.

Um When I'm having these conversations, I'm hearing, we're trying to condense the sources we're using, we're trying to expand the ones that are working and we really want to get like a handful of partners.
Um Like true partners.
Do you feel like that resonates with you guys?
Um Yeah, I feel like it does.
Um I feel like our approach always is to vet out, you know, something first and then if it works well, then we'll roll it out.
I mean, other things, maybe not marketing because it just depends on the asset and occupancy and the leads that are already coming in.
I mean, if you're already getting enough leads, then you don't really need to add any marketing dollars because you have enough leads.
It's really a different issue.
Um If you're not getting enough lead, yes, we would, you know, we rolled out your company and we loved it and it was working well, then yes, we would utilize it on other assets.
But as far as partnering, yes, we like to partner, but again, it's just gonna be on a property by property.
We have a lot of manage properties and we have a lot of fee managed and when we manage it kind of depends on the ownership group.
Totally makes sense.
What percentage of the portfolio is, fee managed.
It's almost half and half.
I think it's like, is it Chris 4656 or something right now?
Yeah, I would say it's like half and half.
Ok.

No, we don't treat anything different.
We treat them all like they're our own personal property honestly.
Now we're forced to treat some of the asset differently if we have an asset manager that wants to roll out something different, right?
So like we have a couple of tides properties and our asset manager is very adamant about rolling out certain products and so we have to follow his lead.
Yeah.
So, so you're really thinking property by property need, whether that's an asset that you own or manage?
Ok, cool.
And then uh what are you like, what's your team's main goals this year?
What are you guys focusing on?
Is it, is it truly increasing leads?
Um Are you looking at, do you have other strategies that you're looking at this year to roll out.
I mean, our main goal is occupancy and we want all the properties to be like 95% and above on occupancy,
I 100% so that I don't get any phone calls.
Sorry, say that again, I wanna be 100% of all properties so I get no phone calls.
Yeah.
There you go.
What are you guys sitting at as, uh, as of right now?
That's a great question.

We have a to d properties.
We have, we taken on distress to where we're trying to get them above 90%.
Then we have, you know, great properties that just kind of 95 and they're gliding.
Yeah, I feel like we just, it's very um very diverse across the portfolio.
Yeah.
Ok.
So, so it sounds like there's an agreement that the, the number one goal right now is occupancy.
Um So I will shy away from the Google product um and stick just towards how we can help um with leads and what I think would be a best fit there.
I'm good.
All right.

What you've what you're doing, what you've done.

Yes.
Ok.
So I will back up because this one does feel relevant.
Um, this is what we're hearing.
We're hearing.
Uh, market has, was stable for a good few years.
We had some really strong years and now it seems that occupancy is sliding really across the country, um, with some markets obviously performing better than others.

Um, and what we're hearing and what we have heard is the old marketing plans aren't really working as well as they used to old marketing plans being let's invest into uh dollars spent when we need it.
And that's typically when occupancy has slid.
So when there's a fire that needs to be put out, typically, that's when we would get phone calls, like, hey, we have these five properties that really need a boost like yesterday.
Uh And we're trying to get to a place where that's not the case where we have consistent advertising throughout the year.
So you don't have to come to us or apartments.com with, hey, we need to put this property on our highest tier because it's struggling, right?
So what what we're offering is we have and I'll ask Christy, have you met with us in the past?
Are you familiar with rentable?
No, I'm not.
Ok.

Um And this also does include targeted social ads.
So I'll jump into what all of that means.
But what are you guys doing as far as social, like, targeted social media ads go right now
or sponsored ads?
I don't think we really are.
Is that something that you guys are looking into or have talked about?

So, but I don't think we've, I mean, maybe we've looked into it.
I know Chris does a little bit more than I do on that side of it.

of that or maybe like Facebook marketplace, you know, we've done in house.

So it's not, this tool is not managing anything on your community level pages.
Um But it's, it's running sponsored paid ads.
So the difference, obviously, I'm sure, you know, but the difference is that what you're doing to these is the reach is much greater.
So your property level pages are only reaching your audience that follows you.
Um or that's friends with your page where these are targeted, sponsored ads that run in the news feed um of both Facebook and Instagram.
Um And we're thinking about this in a, a little bit different way whereas we are talking with other companies who are doing these internally where they're paying.
Um So typically that is we're paying per ad, you're paying um per boost that you send out.

We're taking the spend that you spend towards these and um splitting it up across the month so that there's consistent ads going out throughout the entire month versus just a boost here and a boost there.
Um So jumping back a little bit, so the, the entire package includes the rentable platform.
So that's our website and then we syndicate out to a large amount of partner sites.
Um We're actually reaching about 80% of renters searching across the entire ils ecosystem.
Um Minus the, the large one here that you're already partnering with.
Um We're reaching most of the other sites.
OK.

The last piece of this is we have an A I tool um that kind of runs all of this.
So you might be wondering, OK, like how do we get out to, out to all of these sites.
So this, this tool that we have in place can read how much traffic you're getting on each site that we syndicate to by property.
And then we'll push to the sites that are working better by property.

So say you have one property that's doing really well on the Facebook ads and realtor.com, we'll make sure that we're putting most of the spend that we have for that property towards those sites and taking it away from maybe like walk score that isn't performing as well.
And that's always running and it's always optimizing which sites um are getting the most spend.

And is smart.
What are your thoughts on this?

This product?

The way to scale the pricing down is by volume.

How many properties are you guys at now?
five?

perfect.

or like ratio, I think more.

Yeah.

Um But it all everything else is just location based and it, yes, it is just around the property.
Um And I'd say the difference between like a typical geo fencing company is that we are just doing the, the geo fencing on target social.

I think, probably six months.
Ok.
Yeah.
Ok.

with geo fencing, will these ads only pop up if that prospect is like inside our leasing office searching on their Facebook or will it pop up like later on when they're at home on their Facebook?

Yes.
Yeah.

We'll extend it.

Yeah.

that.

So that's how we are creating the rentable listing.
Um So we're able to pull pricing availability, photos, amenity based information all directly from Intrata.
That's how the rentable listing gets created.
And then the targeted social ads are created off of the rentable listing um which is integrated.

Um And then it's an a I tool that creates the content itself, but it's all based off of your feed data.

So you guys are also pulling property specials from intro
if you have it at intro.

Yep.
OK.
Yeah, we
do.
Yeah.

It's you turn on integration to us from intrata, uh We turn it on from there.
Um And then really the only manual thing that needs to be done is us getting the list of where the, where the leads should go.
Um So whether that's you want us to send them back to Intrata as a guest card or you want them sent as a lead, we would just need that lead email.
Um And then it's pretty automated from there.

And if we, if we were to bring on, let's say five properties with you up front, um Would you be willing to give us better pricing on those in hopes that we like the service and then um eventually expand that to more properties.
I would say if we get to more like the 10 property mark, um I can drop down to the next tier here.
Let's go.
If, if we can get to eight properties, I can go to 149.
Um But at the five that's on the low side of this one, I would have to stay at the 199.
Ok, good to know.
Ok, that sounds good.
Ok, and I'll include that in an email too.

We talked um eight plus properties we can do 149.
Yeah.

I mean, I know it's kind of like a random question but is it kind of like immediate like if we, we were to sign up tomorrow and it in, is it immediately pull everything over from entrada tomorrow and start posting ads tomorrow?
Yeah, so this tool is pretty quick.
I would say implementation is the turn on for implementation itself is a few days, a few business days.
Um So if we got a contract signed today, I would say it's very likely to be live by the end of the week if not like Monday at the latest.
Um But these ads do start working right away.
So there's not like a ramp up time like on a like on an organic Google tool.
Um It's, it's immediate.
So the ads start running right away.
OK.
Awesome.
Good to know.
Yeah.
Right.
I don't think I have any more questions.
Um
Right.

Um I don't know, to be honest, um I typically will have conversations with my ownership group.

So, I mean, it's kind of all in their hands at that point, whether they jump on board and they're like, hey, let's do it and it's immediate or if they want us to be out other companies that um are equivalent to this one just to get the pros and the cons.
Yeah, that makes it sorry.
I don't have an exact.
No, that's ok.
I just wanna make sure I'm following up appropriately.
I don't wanna message you tomorrow asking if you have a decision if it's gonna take, you know, a week or two or something like that.

So, learn about all the different products and learn what they have to offer so that we can make our best decision ba you know, per property based on what we've learned.

of course.

Ok?
